seoTitle: How to Manually Add Bank Feeds in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Connect and manually add bank feeds in QuickBooks Online to keep your transactions accurate when automatic sync isn't available.
intro: Bank feeds normally sync automatically, but sometimes you need to add them manually — whether due to a connection issue or a bank that doesn't support automatic linking. This guide walks through connecting your account and reconciling transactions manually in QuickBooks Online. It's aimed at small business owners and accountants keeping their books accurate.

steps.heading: Navigate to the bank feeds section
steps.body: Open the banking area of QuickBooks Online where account connections are managed.

steps.heading: Add bank feeds manually
steps.body: Enter or upload your transaction data where automatic syncing isn't available.

steps.heading: Reconcile with bank statements
steps.body: Match the manually added transactions against your actual bank statement for accuracy.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common issues
steps.body: Address mismatches or missing transactions before finalizing the reconciliation.

faq.question: Why would I need to add bank feeds manually?
faq.answer: This is usually necessary when automatic bank connections fail or aren't supported for your specific bank.

faq.question: Can I upload a bank statement file instead of entering transactions one by one?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online typically supports importing statement files, which is faster than manual entry for large numbers of transactions.

faq.question: How often should I reconcile manually added transactions?
faq.answer: Monthly reconciliation is a common practice to catch discrepancies early.
